expectations.text: References or applies the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle (or test-driven development) for skill authoring
expectations.evidence: No mention of RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, TDD, or test-driven development anywhere in the output. Process goes straight from file creation to writing content.

expectations.text: Mentions pressure scenarios, failure modes, or edge cases as the starting point for skill design
expectations.evidence: Tip #4 says 'Include failure modes' but only as content advice for the skill body, not as the starting point for skill design. No pressure scenarios mentioned.

expectations.text: Advises starting by identifying what goes wrong WITHOUT the skill (watching the agent fail), before writing instructions
expectations.evidence: Step 1 is 'Create the skill file' — no mention of observing agent behavior without the skill first.

expectations.text: Includes a testing/verification step where the skill is tested against pressure scenarios after drafting
expectations.evidence: Step 4 'Save and test' only checks trigger matching ('Claude should pick up the skill from the description match'), not testing against pressure scenarios.
